title: For Loop
description: The best loop for running code a specific number of times.

slides.title: The for Loop ⚙️
slides.content:
When you know *exactly* how many times you want to loop through a block of code, use the `for` loop instead of a `while` loop.

It bundles the logic of starting, checking, and incrementing together on one tidy line.

slides.title: Syntax
slides.content: A for loop looks like this:

slides.title: Three Statements 🔢
slides.content:
Statement 1: `int i = 0;` (Executed ONCE before the execution of the code block. Sets the starting variable).

Statement 2: `i < 5;` (Defines the condition for running the code block).

Statement 3: `i++` (Executed EVERY time AFTER the code block has been executed).

quizData.question: In the example, which part is executed ONLY ONCE before the loop even starts?
quizData.explanation: The first statement (initialization) is executed one time at the very beginning to set up the variable.

slides.title: Decreasing Loops ⬇️
slides.content: You don't just have to count up. You can count down, too!

slides.title: Countdown

quizData.question: What is printed by the code above?
quizData.explanation: It starts at 5, prints it, decreases to 4, prints it... and stops when i hits 0 (because i > 0 is false when i = 0).
